instruction: Add a private Google Sheet review queue for persisted evidence. Do not add new sources, Bedrock drafting, subscriber delivery or report scheduling in this stage.

workbook.rule: Use one private workbook with protected subscriber data. Do not mix subscriber rows into the evidence table.

technical_requirements: Use a Google service account or equivalent server-to-server credential
Store credentials in SSM Parameter Store SecureString, never in code
Share only the private workbook with the service account
Upsert by stable item_id so repeat runs do not create duplicate rows
Treat Sheets failure as a recorded partial failure after DynamoDB/S3 persistence succeeds
Make the integration feature-disabled by default until configured
Keep DynamoDB and S3 as the source of truth
security_requirements: Do not create a public sheet
Do not log service-account JSON, access tokens, sheet IDs or subscriber data
Do not create or modify the subscribers tab in this stage
Do not use a personal OAuth refresh token in Lambda
tests: row mapping preserves item_id, source, URL, dates and review defaults
upsert is idempotent for the same item_id
Sheets failure does not discard persisted evidence
missing configuration disables the integration safely
credentials are read from the configured secret source without logging values
stop_after: Show the sheet schema and credential flow before bulk implementation if either is ambiguous. After implementation, return files, tests, deployment parameters and risks, then stop.
